entry.title: Re: Bem Vindos ao Grupo de Salvador da Comunidade SAP!
entry.content:
Olá Aline! Olá Comunidade SAP de Salvador!

Primeiro, quero agradecer a Aline por abrir este espaço que estava faltando para os gurus SAP de Salvador 🙂

Eu aproveito para também me apresentar: Eu sou Emmanuel Halbout, consultor SAP técnico polivalente / Desenvolvedor fullstack SAP: ABAP OO, UI5, BC, Workflow, ... Participei de muitos projetos de integração, implantação, upgrade, pela Europa, e pelo mundo, e tenho experiência e conhecimento com vários módulos : MM, SD, LE, PP, QM, WM, EWM. Sou colaborador da HRC Consulting, empresa francesa de consultoria SAP, de 80 colaboradores, que atua em vários setores e módulos, com foco especial na supply-chain e no desenvolvimento de aplicativos móveis para SAP.

Minha vida é meio atípica pois está compartilhada entre a França e a Bahia (~6 meses aqui, ~6 meses lá). Aqui em Salvador estou morando no Rio Vermelho, com minha esposa e minha filha. Minha esposa trabalha com o NEOJIBA, um programa social do governo da Bahia. Eu sou jogador de vôlei de praia, e também gosto de nadar, correr e pedalar.

Voltando a SAP, a maioria dos clientes com quem estou trabalhando são europeus, mas eu quero conhecer outros atores de SAP aqui na Bahia. O povo baiano é o mais acolhedor e mais caloroso que tem. Seria uma alegria conhecer vocês, e, quem sabe, colaborar ou criar amizades !

Sinto muito por não ter participado do SAP Inside track 2025, mas naquele momento tive que dar foco na nossa criatura recém-nascida 🙂

Vou deixar aqui meu endereço e-mail, e se vocês gostariam de conversar, de compartilhar, sobre assunto SAP ou outros, de se encontrar, por favor me escrevam    ===>   emmanuel.halbout@hrc-consulting.fr

Até logo !! A bientôt !!

Emmanuel

entry.title: Mastering Complex SBPA Approvals : A Deep Dive into Wait Steps, Script Tasks, and API Integrations
entry.content:
Welcome back to our new blog series, where we previously delved into the intricacies of dynamic sequential approval workflows.

In this two-part series, my colleague @Adwait_Fadnavis  and I will explore some very critical business workflow  requirements  in detail using SAP Build Process Automation. 

We will demonstrate how to use various SAP Build Process Automation tools to solve real-time challenges effectively.

This blog post addresses this complex requirement with detailed step-by-step instructions. You'll learn how to ensure workflows advance smoothly, even with  approval condition utilizing various innovative latest features such as: Wait Step with API Triggering , Workflow Context update via API etc.

Business Context and Problem Statement

While working on multiple SAP Build Process Automation projects, we have faced situations where workflows only proceed if all departments or approvers approve them unanimously.

If any approver rejects the proposal, the entire workflow is rejected . This creates a need for a parallel approval process, adhering to an "all-or-nothing" requirement, which SAP's standard solutions do not support.

In a below sample procurement workflow, the request is created and submitted. Both the Department Manager and Finance Department must approve it simultaneously. If either rejects, the workflow is terminated.

If both approve, the request goes to the Executive for final approval. The Executive's decision determines whether the request is processed or terminated.

Technical Solutioning & Design

In this process, we will create two workflows using SAP Build Process Automation’s Low Code/No Code approach.

Let’s refer to these as Workflow A (main workflow) and Workflow B.

Workflow A( Main Workflow): This is the main workflow, triggered with all input parameters, responsible for the overall business process flow.

Workflow B(Secondary Workflow): This workflow handles the approval task. It receives the Approval ID, total number of levels, and current level. If the final approval is granted, it triggers a wait step API  in Workflow A to proceed to the next level. If more approvals are needed, it sends the Approval ID to the next level.

 
Technical Design Steps for Workflow A:
 
  1. Trigger Workflow: The workflow is initiated with input parameters.
  2. Decision Creation: A decision step is created to determine the number of approvals require. 

3. Script Task: This step determines the total number of approvals and the current approval level.

$.context.custom.doneapprovalcount = 0;
$.context.custom.currentcount = 0;
$.context.custom.subwfdefinationid = "Your Secondaray Workflow ID"; // replace with your secondary workflow id
$.context.custom.totalapprovalcount = $.context.decision_getAppprovers_1.Approvers.length;
$.context.custom.triggeredsubprocessids = [];
​
  • Condition Check: If the current level matches the total required approvals, A wait step will be created to wait for the completion. If not, Workflow B is triggered to initiate the approval task.

  • Trigger Workflow B: Workflow B sends the work item to the respective user for approval.
  • Wait Step API : This critical step waits for all parallel approvals to be completed. Once done, the dummy decision is triggered by Workflow B. This is very interesting and latest features
    1. Create a Wait Step with API as follows. After Deployment it will event will create the API End Point which will used to trigger from secondary workflow B .      
    2. Execution ID is the Parent Workflow ID.
    3. Upload the API specification and create the action for this
    4.  Also make sure to create the API key which will be used to trigger it from secondary workflow B.

Technical Design Steps for Workflow B  (Triggered via Workflow A)

  1. Trigger from Workflow A : Workflow B starts when Workflow A reaches a certain point to manage approval tasks separately and in parallel to the main workflow.

  2. Send Approval Task: The approval task is sent to the current level approver to obtain necessary approvals at each level before proceeding.
  3. On Approval: The system retrieves instance details and updates the approval count to keep track of completed approvals and update the workflow status.  
  4. sample code
$.context.custom.approvedcount = $.context.action_get_getV1WorkflowInstancesWorkflowInstanceIdContext_6.result.custom.doneapprovalcount + 1;
  • Check Count: The system checks if all required approvals are obtained:
    • If all approvals are done, the workflow proceeds to the next step.
    • If not all approvals are done This ensures the workflow only proceeds once all approvals are obtained
  •  Update Parent Workflow A Approval Action : increment the update count. created a custom action. If all the approvals not done, then update the approval count via custom action. (refer our previous blog how to create action for workflow API)   
  • Retrieve Task ID & Trigger Wait API trigger wait step ( API) in Workflow A to signal that the approval process is complete / Reject  and it can proceed.
    • Now come to most crucial part of the Development: Trigger the wait step via API
    • Created a custom action project  for the wait step api end point.
    • Here is the Input mapping for the API call. Needs to provide the API Key and Parent ID.
    • It will trigger the wait step in workflow A to approve / move forward the workflow . 
 
In the upcoming blog post, @Adwait_Fadnavis  will delve into the complete end to end testing for these which is very critical to understand the whole development flow.
 
Stay tuned for a comprehensive guide that will enhance your understanding and streamline your Development  process.

Regards,

Mohit Bansal,

SAP Mentor. SAP BTP Solution Architect

entry.title: Re: Incorrect Cost on Partial Finished Goods Receipt in P...
entry.content:
Hi,

In standard configuration, the finished goods should be valued at Standard Price and the goods receipt should use this to valuate the quantity received from the order. Please check the material price. With standard price the values posted onto the order are not relevant for valuating the finished goods stock on receipt to inventory.

entry.title: Re: Story Changes Lost in Edit Mode Due to Active Bookmark in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Thank you, William.

I’ve been able to consistently reproduce the issue and would like to clarify the behavior:

  • After making formatting changes (such as text color, font size, font style, layout, etc.) and saving the story, the changes are correctly saved.
  • However, when the story is later opened in View Mode, it seems that a Bookmark is automatically applied, which reverts the story to an older formatting state.
  • When switching to Edit Mode, the story still reflects the Bookmark state. If this goes unnoticed and the story is saved again, it appears as though the recent formatting changes are lost.

This behavior can be confusing, especially when users are unaware that a Bookmark is being auto-applied at runtime.

I’ll proceed to raise a support ticket as you suggested. In the meantime, it would be helpful to know if there’s a way to prevent automatic Bookmark loading or to default to the last saved state when opening a story.

Thanks again for your guidance!

entry.title: Re: Key-user extensibility and updating fields
entry.content: The field is indeed added there, but you can't update it via that object (in EML, or a OData call)

entry.title: Re: Key-user extensibility and updating fields
entry.content:
@patrick_winkler,

Isn't that a big limitation on key-user extensibility? Won't the key user expect to be able to update the field? It seems to me that it will almost always be the case that the user will want to be able to update the field that he has added via that app. And so that key-user extensibility should support updating of the field from the frontend Fiori app (and so also from the backend via EML on the same object that is called from the frontend).

Thomas

entry.title: How to Improve Paginated Queries Performances
entry.content:
Overview

We've seen many customers implementing custom cronjob to process large volumes of data using paginated queries to reduce memory footprint when results are loaded in memory.

Using traditional and standard SAP Commerce Cloud pagination technique, called offset pagination, can lead to severe performance issues which are explained and overcome in the next sections.

This article presents and compares various pagination approaches.

Offset Pagination

There are various SAP Commerce API API supporting paginated queries, the sample Groovy code below shows how it can be implemented using PaginatedFlexibleSearchService  to process Product data:

import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.FlexibleSearchQuery
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.paginated.PaginatedFlexibleSearchParameter
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.paginated.PaginatedFlexibleSearchService
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.paginated.util.PaginatedSearchUtils
import org.apache.commons.lang.time.StopWatch
  
def pageSize = 10
 
def paginatedParameter = new PaginatedFlexibleSearchParameter()
paginatedParameter.flexibleSearchQuery = new FlexibleSearchQuery("SELECT {pk} FROM {Product}")
paginatedParameter.searchPageData = PaginatedSearchUtils.createSearchPageDataWithPagination(pageSize, 0, true)
 
def stopWatch = new StopWatch()
stopWatch.start()
 
do {
    paginatedParameter.searchPageData = paginatedFlexibleSearchService.search(paginatedParameter)
    // Process searchPageData
    [...]
    paginatedParameter.searchPageData.pagination.currentPage = paginatedParameter.searchPageData.pagination.currentPage + 1
} while (paginatedParameter.searchPageData.pagination.hasNext)
 
stopWatch.stop()
println "Offset Pagination elapsed time in ${stopWatch}"

 This query will typically be translated into the following SQL with a technique named offset pagination as shown in the JDBC logs:

1624|master|250219-10:14:23:207|1 ms|statement|...|SELECT  item_t0.PK  FROM products item_t0 WHERE (item_t0.TypePkString IN  (8796103508050,8796103475282,8796103213138,8796103344210,8796103442514,8796103278674,8796103245906,8796103311442,8796103409746,8796098986066) ) ORDER BY 1 OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY
1624|master|250219-10:14:23:210|3 ms|statement|...|SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT FROM ( SELECT  item_t0.PK  FROM products item_t0 WHERE (item_t0.TypePkString IN  (8796103508050,8796103475282,8796103213138,8796103344210,8796103442514,8796103278674,8796103245906,8796103311442,8796103409746,8796098986066) ) ) CNTTABLEALIAS
1624|master|250219-10:14:23:210|0 ms|statement|...|SELECT  item_t0.PK  FROM products item_t0 WHERE (item_t0.TypePkString IN  (8796103508050,8796103475282,8796103213138,8796103344210,8796103442514,8796103278674,8796103245906,8796103311442,8796103409746,8796098986066) ) ORDER BY 1 OFFSET 10 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY
1624|master|250219-10:14:23:213|3 ms|statement|...|SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT FROM ( SELECT  item_t0.PK  FROM products item_t0 WHERE (item_t0.TypePkString IN  (8796103508050,8796103475282,8796103213138,8796103344210,8796103442514,8796103278674,8796103245906,8796103311442,8796103409746,8796098986066) ) ) CNTTABLEALIAS
1624|master|250219-10:14:23:213|0 ms|statement|...|SELECT  item_t0.PK  FROM products item_t0 WHERE (item_t0.TypePkString IN  (8796103508050,8796103475282,8796103213138,8796103344210,8796103442514,8796103278674,8796103245906,8796103311442,8796103409746,8796098986066) ) ORDER BY 1 OFFSET 20 ROWS FETCH NEXT 10 ROWS ONLY
1624|master|250219-10:14:23:216|2 ms|statement|...|SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT FROM ( SELECT  item_t0.PK  FROM products item_t0 WHERE (item_t0.TypePkString IN  (8796103508050,8796103475282,8796103213138,8796103344210,8796103442514,8796103278674,8796103245906,8796103311442,8796103409746,8796098986066) ) ) CNTTABLEALIAS

 

Offset pagination can lead to severe performance issues as pages are processed towards the end of large page set.

This is due to the way OFFSET is implemented and well explained in the following blog post SQL Pagination Techniques: Enhancing Query Performance and Managing Memory Efficiently (Medium) :

Memory and Performance Considerations:

  • For small offsets, this method is usually fast and consumes minimal memory.
  • As the offset grows, the query can become slow and consume more memory because the database engine has to process and discard the rows up to the offset.

The complexity of such pagination technique is quadratic in the number of pages n because it is a factor of ∑i = n * (n+1) / 2.

The diagram below shows how the query response increases over time in such a way :

Keyset pagination

As described in the blog post, a much more efficient technique for pagination is keyset validation, where the last fetched value is used as a reference for the next query instead of using OFFSET. This technique is more efficient because it avoids the need to scan rows that were already fetched.

Of course to be really efficient, you must ensure the columns used for ordering and filtering are indexed.

In the case above we can use the pk  column for such purpose and refactor the code as follow :

import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.FlexibleSearchQuery
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.paginated.PaginatedFlexibleSearchParameter
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.paginated.PaginatedFlexibleSearchService
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.search.paginated.util.PaginatedSearchUtils
import org.apache.commons.lang.time.StopWatch
  
def pageSize = 10
 
def paginatedParameter = new PaginatedFlexibleSearchParameter()
paginatedParameter.flexibleSearchQuery = new FlexibleSearchQuery("SELECT {pk} FROM {Product} WHERE {pk} > ?lastPk ORDER BY {pk} ASC")
paginatedParameter.searchPageData = PaginatedSearchUtils.createSearchPageDataWithPagination(pageSize, 0, false)
def lastPk = -1l
 
def stopWatch = new StopWatch()
stopWatch.start()
do {
    paginatedParameter.flexibleSearchQuery.addQueryParameter("lastPk", lastPk)
    paginatedParameter.searchPageData = paginatedFlexibleSearchService.search(paginatedParameter)
    // Process searchPageData
    [...]
    if (!paginatedParameter.searchPageData.results.isEmpty())
        lastPk = paginatedParameter.searchPageData.results.last().pk.longValue
} while (paginatedParameter.searchPageData.results.size() == pageSize)
 
stopWatch.stop()
println "Keyset Pagination elapsed time in ${stopWatch}"

Here we have set the needsTotal parameter for PaginatedSearchUtils#createSearchPageDataWithPagination to force the offset to be 0 on each iteration as shown in the JDBC logs :

1625|master|250219-10:08:28:382|1 ms|statement|...|SELECT  item_t0.PK  FROM products item_t0 WHERE ( item_t0.PK  > -1) AND (item_t0.TypePkString IN  (8796103508050,8796103475282,8796103213138,8796103344210,8796103442514,8796103278674,8796103245906,8796103311442,8796103409746,8796098986066) ) order by  item_t0.PK  ASC OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 11 ROWS ONLY
1625|master|250219-10:08:28:384|1 ms|statement|...|SELECT  item_t0.PK  FROM products item_t0 WHERE ( item_t0.PK  > 8796093349889) AND (item_t0.TypePkString IN  (8796103508050,8796103475282,8796103213138,8796103344210,8796103442514,8796103278674,8796103245906,8796103311442,8796103409746,8796098986066) ) order by  item_t0.PK  ASC OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 11 ROWS ONLY
1625|master|250219-10:08:28:386|1 ms|statement|...|SELECT  item_t0.PK  FROM products item_t0 WHERE ( item_t0.PK  > 8796093677569) AND (item_t0.TypePkString IN  (8796103508050,8796103475282,8796103213138,8796103344210,8796103442514,8796103278674,8796103245906,8796103311442,8796103409746,8796098986066) ) order by  item_t0.PK  ASC OFFSET 0 ROWS FETCH NEXT 11 ROWS ONLY

Offset vs. Keyset Pagination

On a small set of Product (7,314), we see a 60% improvement with keyset pagination when running above scripts :

Offset Pagination elapsed time in 0:00:02.709
Keyset Pagination elapsed time in 0:00:01.056

Quick Win - Increase Page Size

Another way of looking at this is to estimate the real impact on memory with pagination. For queries projecting on pk only, at the end of each iteration you will end up having all PK for a given page allocated in the heap. PK are stored as long  attribute whose size is 8 bytes in memory on 64 bit CPU.

This means that with a page size of 1 million, the memory footprint will be approximately 8 MB when all rows in the page result are iterated , which is very much bearable both in terms of required memory and GC throughput. Of course you will have to ensure this by monitoring JVMheap metrics & usage.

 

So a quick win to fix such issue can be very simple : increase the page size to a very large and acceptable number to reduce the impact of offset validation.

entry.title: ARIBA API data extraction & reporting with SAP BTP
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post I'm sharing some highlights from a recent project work involving data extraction from ARIBA API into SAP Datasphere OpenSQL schema using Integration suite with a basic dashboard in Analytics cloud. 

        ✅ Use Integration Suite iflow to extract data from ARIBA API.

        ✅ Persisting records in Datasphere OpenSQL schema

        ✅ Data modelling in Datasphere

        ✅ Reporting in Analytics Cloud

Upcoming Functionality with SAP BDC 🚀

With the annoucement of SAP Business Data cloud (BDC), a fully managed SaaS solution that unifies and governs all SAP data and seamlessly connects with third-party data, we will soon have standard Data Products & Insight Applications across SAP LoB solutions, and with that we will have direct access to such data assets without the need for data integration as in this blog post. SAP has already started publishing these data products and these can be looked up here : SAP Data Products.

In this post you get to see some highlights with screenshots on how SAP Integration suite can work with ARIBA APIs, with end to end data modelling and visualization. Its quite relevant for customers with exisitng subscription of Integration Suite. 

SAP Discovery Mission 🔍

For detailed implemenation of ARIBA API spend data extraction please refer this SAP Discovery mission which comes with Integration Suite iflows, sample SQL scripts to execute on Datasphere etc. 

While the discovery mission is very detailed , we will observe in the blog post the overall flow with sample implementation steps using a single iflow and I hope it will help understand the overall flow when modelling ARIBA apis for extraction. 

ARIBA API ⛓

I have used the Purchase orders buyer API which can tested using the free sandbox at api.sap.com 

ARIBA Network Purchase Orders buyer API

Prepackaged Integration Content 🎁

With Integration Suites prepackaged content you get well designed iflows which you can configure for your specific systems & apis.

Explore Prepakaged Content on Business Accelerator Hub .

For the integration in discussion as well we have the standard iflows listed as below:

ARIBA connection iflows on Business Accelerator Hub

On your integration suite tenant you can access this prepackaged content under Discover --> integrations 

Published ARIBA connection package in Integration suite : 

As part of this packages you get 2 iflows as described below : 

MODIFIED INTEGRATION FLOW (iflow): ↖️

I did some modifications on this prepackaged iflow so that I can connect to ARIBA and then process data using below addtional operators : 

  • General Splitter
  • Content modifier
  • JDBC receiver (OpenSQL schema of SAP Datasphere) 

If needed you can download this from my Github page : Download modified iflow

Here is a screenshot of the modified iflow : 

So the second iflow "output_from_ARIBA" is simply calling the first iflow using ProcessDirect and it gets deployed with an HTTP end point, which when queried triggers the first iflow "Connect SAP ARIBA to the SAP ARIBA APIs". The iflows are used to just show the mechanism of fast and direct communication between iflows using Process Direct, you can read more it here : ProcessDirect 

In the screenshot you see that "output_from_ARIBA" iflow gets deployed with an address of /vinay/Ariba, this will reflect in the eventual endpoint when the iflow is deployed. 

 

Below we see the processDirect Adapter making an internal call to the first iflow, which gets deployed with an address : /Ariba/OpenAPI.

 

Extract data from ARIBA API ▶️

Lets now look at the first iflow "connect SAP Ariba to the SAP Ariba APIs" in detail. We will go through each important section of the iflow and understand the processing with screen shots. 

GET ACCESS TOKEN

The first step in the iflow execution is to get an Access token from ARIBA's Oauth URL with encoded client id/secret, an auth token is returned which is then saved in the header.

 

 

GET DOCUMENT

Now when then access token is retrieved we can further add additional headers for our subsequent call to ARIBA's Purchase order buyer API, e.g in this case the ARIBA networkx- ID along with the API key. 

Now we make the final HTTP request to ARIBA API, in the query ARIBA Realm and filters can also be specified. 

JSON TO XML Converter

The JSON structure of the API was intitially tested at the api.sap.com, and now we have used JSON to XML converter, so the message can be parsed using the XPATH - which is a query language to navigate through elements and attributes in an XML document.

GENERAL SPLITTER

Splitter will simply split the XML message based on the number of records as in this example there will be multiple purchase orders returned from the ARIBA API. 

CONTENT MODIFIER

In the message body of the Content Modifier, we now use Type Expression, to build a SQL query using the exchange properties we desgined above. 

Finally, we reach the point of the database call which is configured as the OpenSQL schema of the Datasphere. 

The JDBC connection is maintained in the integration suite tenant as below : 

Runnning the Integration Flow  

We deploy both iflows and we can see the Endpoint against the iflow below : 

Call the endpoint either using Postman or visual Studio code and you will see the message processed as below : 

We now verify the OpenSQL table to see the persisted records : ( Please note we only had a few records from the sabdbox API used in this case and the iflow was run mutiple times so you see some duplicate records below)

 

 

Data modelling in Datasphere 📈 📊

With the data moved to Datasphere OpenSQL schema we can access the table in Datasphere Data Builder, Open a new Graphical view and from the left navigation under sources you would see the linked openSQL Schema. Drag & drop the table on the canvas. I have added a calculated column here to convert the POAMOUNT to a decimal value ( it was nvarchar after the initial data import). Save and deploy the view.

 

Create an Analytical model using the Graphical view created above, use the POAMOUNT field as a Measure : 

Reporting in SAP Analytics Cloud 📊

I'm using a linked SAP analytics cloud tenant, you can learn how to connect Datasphere & Analytics cloud tenants here.

Once linked, open a black story and proceed to add new data --> Existing Data model --> select Datasphere system from the left navgation as below.

Select your deployed model under the datasphere space name and build a story with data that you processed using the iflow : 

 

CONCLUSION 🔚

Hope this helps the community members to get an overall idea of the integration discussed in this blog post and sparks ideas for your exisitng or future projects. 

Thank you for your time & attention! 

APPENDIX 

Monitoring the iflow and the message flow using trace

As some of you must already know Integration suite provides several advanced capabilities to monitor the iflow. In the below steps we will enable the trace on the main iFlow and see how different components are workign together : 

Enabling the Trace : 

Navigate to Monitor --> Integrations and APIs --> Manage Integration Content and look for the "Started" iflows for ARIBA.

Here we enable the Trace by changing the "Log Level" to trace. The trace only gets enabled for 10 mins (default setting) as Integration suite startes storing on the messages. 

Trigger the iFlow again to fetch the records and so that we can now see the tracing results : 

Goto : Monitor --> Integrations and APIs --> Monitor Message Processing 

you can now see the trace as below, click on the "Trace" :

 

Now, we can easily check various parameters, headers, exchange properties & payloads across various the iflow: 

You can see the JSON payload coming through below : 

Splitting & XML conversion happens in the iflow and here we see the splitted payload: 

Extracted Exchange properties can be seen here (e.g documentNumber) : 

Finally, we see the prepared SQL statement.

Tracing is a very powerful tool to debug the various phases of the iflow and at the same time allowing us to format the data passing through to make required corrections.

entry.title: Is there any equivalent app to J3RKKRD / J3RKKRS on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system and looking for equivalent applications to the following on-premise programs:

J3RKKRD – Russia: G/L Account Balance

J3RKKRS – Russia: G/L Account Line Items

I’ve already searched in the Fiori App Library and found some apps that seem to have similar functionality. However, when I checked the Business Catalog listed in the library, I couldn’t find any matching catalogs available in our public cloud system for role assignment.

I would like to ask:

Are there standard apps in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that are functionally equivalent to J3RKKRD and J3RKKRS?

If yes, what is the Business Catalog associated with them?

If the apps are available in the library but not in our system, is there any way to activate or request them? 

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to deploy SAP BW/4HANA and SAP BW on a...
entry.content:
The closest product to  Public Cloud version of SAP BW/4HANA is SAP BW Bridge:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW_BRIDGE

 

Yet both SAP BW 7.50 and SAP BW/4HANA have Private Cloud Editions:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-business-data-cloud-faqs/ba-p/14022781

entry.title: Re: Mapping Header Text for OrderRequest
entry.content: I did think of this - however we don't, as standard hold T&C's as header text and would require quite a large process change. We hold the T&C's as standard text to populate the SAPSCRIPT/Adobe forms

entry.title: Alternative Reconciliation Account in FI JE created by SD Billing doc.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

What we need, on SAP PUBLIC CLOUD project, is to create a journal entry from sales invoice, but on a different reconciliation account, than that in masted data of FI customer role.
In CBC, in Automatic Account Determination (100297) there is an option to add accounts to condition table 008.

Problem is, that this is all in terms of available configuration and there is no lead what to do next.
On On-Prem systems there is also additional node for "Reconciliation Account Determination" we can use.

In CBC there is nothing related to those Reconciliation accounts.

Could you please support, is there any Scope Item that would extend CBC options or is there a different approach we should consider?
This blog explains what we would like to achieve https://onlyordinarysapnotes.com/2013/08/26/alternative-reconciliation-account-for-customer/

Thank you and best regards!
Grzegorz

entry.title: Re: Synthetic User Monitoring Query for Supported applica...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

The Synthetic User Monitoring (SUM) in SAP Cloud ALM, it is unlike the other monitoring use cases, we can't find the list of supported products for the SUM.

As documented in the guide: Synthetic User Monitoring - Content:
The Synthetic User Monitoring (SUM) in SAP Cloud ALM relies on Selenium IDE scripts for its scenarios. This gives it the capability to support most of the web applications with some limitations, which are described in the following section.

  • Synthetic User Monitoring can only monitor web applications.
  • Synthetic User Monitoring cannot monitor an application that cannot be operated via a Selenium IDE script.
  • Selenium IDE does not provide any authentication management.
    Therefore, the only supported authentication methods is the "direct authentication" (via a user/password), i.e. by adding in the Selenium IDE script the actions of entering the logon user and its password into a login page.
  • Selenium IDE cannot script web applications relying on Shadow DOM structures. However, in some cases, workarounds to create Selenium IDE scripts exist.
  • The Selenium IDE script must only use commands supported by Synthetic User Monitoring
    see Which Selenium commands does Synthetic User Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM for operations support?
  • The monitored web application must be accessible from the runner endpoint. 

So for the S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, if transactions/applications(eg: webgui) can be accessed with a web browser can be recorded with the Selenium IDE and then it can be monitored in the SUM application.
However, for the actions/transactions on the SAP Logon, it is not supported.

Best regards,
Peng

entry.title: Assistance Required for Assignment Field Update in Vendor Line Item during MIRO Posting
entry.content:
Hi Friends,

Good day!.

We have a requirement to populate the Assignment field (PO Number) in the vendor line item during MIRO posting, using the value from the Assignment field of the GR line item. However, we are currently unable to identify a suitable enhancement spot to implement this change.

Ideally, the Assignment field in the vendor line item should be automatically updated based on the Sort Key maintained in the vendor master data. Unfortunately, this is not happening. The Assignment field is only being populated correctly for the GR line item, based on the GL account"s Sort Key.

We would appreciate your support in either of the following:

  1. Understanding why the Assignment field is not being updated in the vendor line item as expected.
  2. Identifying a suitable enhancement spot, BADI, or user exit where we can implement the required logic.

We have already tried the following BADIs and user exits, but none have worked for our scenario.

BADIs:

 

ACC_DOCUMENT

INVOICE_UPDATE

Exits:

 

LMR1M001

 

EXIT_SAPLMR1M_002

EXIT_SAPLMR1M_003

EXIT_SAPLMRMP_010

LMR1M003

 

EXIT_SAPLMRME_003

LMR1M004

 

EXIT_SAPLMRMP_004

LMR1M005

 

EXIT_SAPLMRMC_002

LMR1M006

 

EXIT_SAPLMRM_BAPI_001

MRMH0001

 

EXIT_SAPLMRMH_001

EXIT_SAPLMRMH_002

MRMH0002

 

EXIT_SAPLMRMH_011

EXIT_SAPLMRMH_012

EXIT_SAPLMRMH_013

EXIT_SAPLMRMH_014

EXIT_SAPLMRMH_015

MRMH0003

 

EXIT_RMMR1MRB_001

EXIT_RMMR1MRB_002

MRMN0001

 

EXIT_SAPLMRMN_001

EXIT_SAPLMRMN_002

EXIT_SAPLMRMN_003

Thanks

Srinivasu

entry.title: SAP BTP Resource Rundown: Top SAP BTP Learning and Enablement Resources
entry.content:
We're thrilled that SAP Business Technology Platform has piqued your interest! As you explore its features, we're here to support you and help you make the most of its capabilities.

We understand that adopting new technology can be a significant step. Our goal is to empower you with strategic implementation tailored to your unique needs, supported by expert guidance every step of the way. That's why we provide an extensive array of trusted resources, personalized just for you.

For you as an SAP BTP Administrator, Developer, or Architect, we offer insights that are relevant to your specific role, with best practices and step-by-step guidance designed to help you overcome business challenges and develop effective solutions. Our top SAP experts have curated key learnings with you in mind, ensuring you have everything you need to succeed.

Let’s dive into a few of the best learning and enablement locations:

Top SAP BTP Information Location #1

A blog post about SAP BTP would simply not be complete without a reference to SAP Discovery Center! It's your go-to place for adopting SAP BTP with practical use cases, detailed guides, and the latest on SAP Business AI. We've gathered expert advice, best practices, and easy-to-follow instructions, turning complicated tasks into simple Missions.

 Start by checking out our Mission Catalog, where you'll find popular Missions packed with pro tips to get you started:

  • Establish a Unified Joule Instance
  • Activate Joule for SAP SuccessFactors
  • Get Started with SAP Build Code and Joule using Generative AI
  • Get Started with Integration Suite

Want more use case insights from SAP Sapphire 2025? Check out the blog post, "Connect, Automate, and Thrive with 4 SAP BTP Use Cases You Cannot Miss at Sapphire 2025".

We’ve covered expert guidance, and if you're curious about costs, the SAP Discovery Center offers two handy estimators—the Service Estimator and the AI Estimator, allowing you to make smart, informed decisions.

Recently released at SAP Sapphire was the new SAP Business AI pricing model. This flexible "Per User Per Month" (PUPM) concept adapts to your needs, letting you scale up or down by users and usage. The AI Estimator is now even better, with details on pricing for Packages, AI Features, and AI Agents.

Explore all SAP Business AI has to offer through our AI Catalog.

Top SAP BTP Guidance Location #2

Welcome to the SAP BTP Guidance Framework! Your central access point to build and run enterprise-grade solutions on SAP BTP. It provides architects, developers and administrators with decision guides, reference architectures, methodologies, security recommendations and DevOps principles.

 Explore a few of the most popular SAP BTP Guidance Framework resources:

  • SAP BTP Developer’s Guide – How you can improve the process of implementing a business application on SAP BTP.  
  • Extension Architecture Guide – How you can evolve your existing extension architecture based on SAP Business Technology Platform and the SAP technology offerings available today.
  • SAP BTP Solution Diagram Repository: The repository includes latest updates and ready-to-use templates to develop high quality diagrams of architectural landscapes.

Two of the newest additions to the SAP BTP Guidance Framework are:

  1. Terraform on SAP BTP: This page serves as a central landing page for all things Terraform and OpenTofu on SAP BTP.
  2. SAP BTP Center of Expertise Guide: This guide helps you set up and expand an SAP BTP Center of Expertise (CoE) with a comprehensive, self-service framework. Start with the operating model to establish or refine your SAP BTP CoE effectively.

Top SAP BTP Learning Location:

SAP Learning: When it comes to gaining knowledge and expanding your horizon, SAP Learning Journeys are top notch. They provide you with expert know-how and allow you to learn and excel at your own pace. They offer a broad variety of learning content, including easy-to-follow lessons, hands-on practice, and expert instructors to gain skills and confidence to tackle real-world challenges. Discover a wide variety of learning journeys featuring the SAP Business Technology Platform and filter them to your needs by level, role, product category and product.

Start learning with one of our featured SAP BTP highlights below:

  • Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Becoming an SAP BTP Solution Architect
  • Administrating SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Getting Started with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
  • Learn the Basics of ABAP Programming on SAP BTP 

In addition to these top learning and enablement locations, you may also be interested in:

  • SAP BTP Garage: Monthly live webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos.
  • SAP BTP Innobytes: Quarterly demo videos and blog posts showcasing the latest SAP BTP innovations.
  • SAP BTP Talk: Monthly podcase offering SAP BTP topic deep dives 

Hope you found this resource rundown helpful! For any comments or feedback, please leave a comment below.

Happy learning!
